tier1.name: Copy-Paste from Stack Overflow
tier1.flavor: It worked on someone else's machine in 2019. Good enough.

tier1.name: Ctrl+F Power User
tier1.flavor: Never reads past the first match.

tier1.name: Free Snack Hoarder
tier1.flavor: Keeps granola bars in every drawer for emergency morale trades.

tier1.name: Office Plant Whisperer
tier1.flavor: The only living thing in the office that's thriving.

tier1.name: Reply-All Enthusiast
tier1.effect: Immediately: every other player gains 1 Burnout; you gain 2 Political Capital.
tier1.flavor: Accidentally cc'd the whole company on a lunch order question. Iconic.

tier1.name: Emotional Support Rubber Duck
tier1.effect: Once per Quarter, take Overtime without gaining Burnout.
tier1.flavor: It just listens. That's more than your manager does.

tier1.name: “Let's Take This Offline”
tier1.effect: +1 Political Capital/round. Once per Quarter, resolve a Mandatory Training without losing next round's Action Point (you still gain the Badge).
tier1.flavor: Bought yourself three weeks. The meeting still happens eventually.

tier1.name: Standing Desk Enthusiast
tier1.effect: +1 Productivity/round. Ignore Burnout gained from Office Chaos cards.
tier1.flavor: Won't stop talking about it, but their back does feel great.

tier1.name: LinkedIn Thought Leader
tier1.flavor: Posted “Hot take: communication is important.” 40,000 impressions.

tier1.name: Regex Whisperer
tier1.flavor: Nobody else can read their code. That's job security, right?

tier1.name: Actually Reads the Documentation
tier1.effect: +1 Productivity/round. Your Projects cost 1 less Productivity (minimum 1).

tier1.name: Master of Small Talk
tier1.flavor: Knows everyone's dog's name. Cannot recall a single deadline.

tier2.name: Scrum Master
tier2.flavor: Facilitates a 45-minute meeting to schedule a 15-minute meeting.

tier2.name: Full-Stack, Full-Burnout
tier2.flavor: Owns the frontend, backend, database, and the on-call phone.

tier2.name: AI Prompt Engineer
tier2.effect: +3 Productivity/round. Each time an Office Chaos card is drawn, flip a coin; on heads, discard 2 Productivity from your bank (a confident hallucination).
tier2.flavor: Confidently hallucinated the entire Q3 roadmap.

tier2.name: Toxic Positivity Certification
tier2.effect: +2 Political Capital/round. You may not take Self-Care actions.
tier2.flavor: Everything is a learning opportunity, including this.

tier2.name: The Brilliant Jerk
tier2.flavor: Ships incredible code. Has made three people cry this quarter.

tier2.name: Master Delegator
tier2.flavor: Has not personally touched a keyboard since 2021.

tier2.name: Buzzword Compiler
tier2.flavor: Synergizes core competencies to leverage actionable takeaways.

tier2.name: Whiteboard Diagram Enthusiast
tier2.effect: +2 Productivity/round. If you have 4+ Skill cards in play, your Projects cost 1 less Productivity.
tier2.flavor: The diagram has 14 boxes. Nobody knows what 6 of them mean.

tier2.name: On-Call Pager Veteran
tier2.effect: +2 Productivity/round. Ignore the first Burnout you'd gain each Quarter.
tier2.flavor: Has slept through three fire drills and one actual fire.

tier2.name: Imposter Syndrome (Actually Very Competent)
tier2.flavor: Is, statistically, doing better than they think.

tier3.name: The Bus Factor of One
tier3.effect: +3 Productivity/round. If you suffer a Burnout Crisis, lose double the usual Political Capital penalty.
tier3.flavor: If they quit, nothing ships again. Ever.

tier3.name: Golden Handcuffs (Fully Vested)
tier3.effect: Ignore all PIP and demotion effects for the rest of the game.
tier3.flavor: The stock options mature in 18 months. So does nothing else.

tier3.name: Rolodex of Every VP
tier3.flavor: Knows a guy. Always knows a guy.

tier3.name: Ships It Friday at 5 PM
tier3.effect: Immediately complete one Project on the Kanban Board at half its listed Productivity cost (round up).
tier3.flavor: The demo gods are watching. Ship it and pray.

tier3.name: The Exit Interview Isn't Scary Anymore
tier3.effect: Immediately remove all your Burnout and gain 1 Compliance Badge.
tier3.flavor: Says everything they wish they'd said in every performance review — on the way out the door.

tier3.name: The Fixer
tier3.effect: Whenever any other player suffers a Burnout Crisis, gain 2 Political Capital.
tier3.flavor: Somehow always available exactly when someone else's project catches fire.

tier3.name: VP Whisperer
tier3.flavor: Gets meetings on the calendar that shouldn't be mathematically possible.

tier3.name: Golden Parachute Clause
tier3.effect: Hold this card face-down in front of you. The next time you would be demoted, discard this instead and ignore the demotion.
tier3.flavor: Negotiated on the way in. Nobody remembers why.

early.name: Update the README (Nobody Reads It Anyway)
Fix the Bug in Production (On a Friday)
Onboard the New Hire
early.flavor: They seem nice. Give it two Quarters.

early.name: Respond to the Simple Support Ticket
early.flavor: It was a browser cache issue. It's always a browser cache issue.

early.name: Write the Sprint Retro Notes
early.flavor: Action items: none of these will happen.

mid.name: Migrate the Legacy Codebase
mid.flavor: Nobody who wrote the original code still works here.

mid.name: Launch the Feature Nobody Asked For
mid.flavor: It tested well with exactly one user: the VP's nephew.

mid.name: Respond to the RFP (Due Tomorrow, Started Today)
mid.flavor: Yes, we can absolutely do all of this by Q2.

mid.name: Survive the Compliance Audit
mid.flavor: Everyone suddenly remembers where the fire extinguishers are.

mid.name: Rebuild the CI/CD Pipeline (Third Time's the Charm)
mid.flavor: This one's permanent. Definitely. For real this time.

mid.name: Run the All-Hands Presentation
mid.flavor: Slides are just screenshots of Slack messages, but with a logo now.

late.name: Ship the Major Redesign Before the Conference
late.flavor: The demo gods are watching. They are not merciful.

late.name: Turn Around the Failing Division
late.flavor: Everyone competent already quietly left. You didn't get the memo in time.

late.name: Land the Whale Client
late.flavor: They want it fully custom, half price, by Friday.

late.name: Lead the Company-Wide Reorganization
late.flavor: Nobody's title changed, but everyone's manager did. Twice.

late.name: Present to the Board (12 Minutes to Justify a Department)
late.flavor: They looked at their phones for eleven of them.

late.name: Negotiate the Vendor Contract (40 Pages of Terms)
late.flavor: Nobody read past page 3. That's where the bad clause is.

evergreen.name: Answer a “Quick Question” on Slack
evergreen.flavor: That was forty-five minutes ago. There are now six people in the thread.
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

evergreen.name: Reduce Technical Debt
evergreen.flavor: Perpetually 80% done. It has always been 80% done. It will always be 80% done.
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

evergreen.name: Untangle the Legacy Spaghetti (One More Time)
evergreen.flavor: Found a comment that says “DO NOT REMOVE, NOT SURE WHY.” Removed it anyway.
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

evergreen.name: Reply to the Jira Comment From Six Months Ago
evergreen.flavor: The person who filed it left the company in Q2. The ticket did not.
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

evergreen.name: Delete the Commented-Out Code From 2019
evergreen.flavor: It's not documentation. It was never documentation.
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

evergreen.name: Un-hardcode the Hardcoded Value
evergreen.flavor: Replaced “prod-server-3” with a config flag that defaults to “prod-server-3.”
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

evergreen.name: Rotate the API Keys You Forgot About
evergreen.flavor: Rotated three keys. Broke a fourth integration nobody remembered existed.
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

evergreen.name: Bump the Node Version (Nothing Breaks. Probably.)
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

evergreen.name: Consolidate the Three Config Files Into One (Now Four)
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

evergreen.name: Archive the Zombie Microservice
evergreen.flavor: Nobody knows what calls it. Everybody's afraid to find out.
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

evergreen.name: Migrate Off the Framework You Migrated To Last Year
evergreen.flavor: The last migration's postmortem recommended this framework.
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

evergreen.name: Squash 40 Commits Into “misc fixes”
evergreen.flavor: git blame now blames everyone equally.
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

evergreen.name: Finally Read the Incident Postmortem Action Items
evergreen.flavor: Item 1: “Add more monitoring.” Filed fourteen months ago. Still open.
evergreen.note: Evergreen: this slot never leaves the Kanban Board. When claimed, discard this card and immediately draw a new card from the Evergreen pool into the same slot (this one may come up again). Exempt from Scope Creep.

events.name: Reply-All Apocalypse
events.effect: Every player gains 1 Burnout.
events.flavor: 247 replies. 246 said “please remove me from this thread.”

events.name: All-Hands Meeting (Could Have Been an Email)
events.effect: Every player loses 1 Productivity from this round's income.
events.flavor: It was, in fact, an email.

events.name: Layoffs Loom (Just a Rumor... Probably)
events.effect: Every player gains 1 Burnout. The First Player gains 1 Political Capital out of sheer relief.
events.flavor: HR scheduled a “quick chat.” Nobody is calm.

events.name: Quiet Quitting Goes Mainstream
events.effect: Any player may decline their Overtime option this round to remove 1 Burnout instead.
events.flavor: Doing exactly what's in the job description. Revolutionary.

events.name: Free Bagel Friday
events.effect: Every player gains 1 Political Capital.
events.flavor: Cinnamon raisin again. Someone doesn't respect the group chat's preferences.

events.name: Fire Drill
events.effect: The player who used Overtime most recently gains 1 Burnout.
events.flavor: Now standing in a parking lot with 200 coworkers you've never seen before.

events.name: Surprise Reorg
events.effect: Everyone returns their Management Style card. Shuffle the Management deck and deal each player a new one.
events.flavor: Nobody's job changed. Everybody's manager did.

events.name: Engagement Survey (Anonymous, Allegedly)
events.effect: Every player gains 1 Political Capital.
events.flavor: Question 14: ‘I feel my work is valued.’ Strongly Disagree.

events.name: The Office Dog Visits
events.effect: Each player may remove 1 Burnout.
events.flavor: Best coworker on the payroll. Unfortunately unpaid.

events.name: New CEO Announced
events.effect: Each player with 5 or more Skill cards in play discards 1 of their choice.
events.flavor: New broom, new org chart, same problems.

events.name: WiFi Dies Mid-Demo
events.effect: The First Player loses 1 Productivity.
events.flavor: The client watched a loading spinner for four minutes.

events.name: Snack Wall Restocked
events.effect: Every player gains 1 Productivity.
events.flavor: Someone's hoarding the good granola bars again.

events.name: The Chair Finally Gets Fixed
events.effect: Every player may remove 1 Burnout.
events.flavor: After 11 months and 4 support tickets. A quiet miracle.

events.name: Casual Friday Goes Full Week
events.effect: Every player gains 1 Political Capital.
events.flavor: Nobody knows what ‘business casual’ means anymore. Everyone's relieved.

events.name: Unlimited PTO (Policy, Not Practice)
events.effect: No player may use the Self-Care action this round.
events.flavor: Technically infinite. Practically, someone will ask why you're taking it.

events.name: Company Offsite in Cancun
events.effect: The First Player gains 2 Productivity. Every other player gains 1 Burnout (FOMO).
events.flavor: Mandatory fun. Fully mandatory.

events.name: IT Outage
events.effect: Skip the Income Phase for every player this round.
events.flavor: The ticket says ‘Priority: Urgent.’ The queue says ‘Position: 214.’

events.name: Late-Night CEO Email
events.effect: Every player gains 1 Burnout.
events.flavor: Sent 11:47 PM. Subject line: ‘Quick thought 💡’

events.name: Company-Wide Chat Outage
events.effect: No player may take the Network action this round.
events.flavor: Turns out everyone was just Slacking each other from three feet away.

events.name: Corporate Wellness Session
events.effect: Every player may remove 1 Burnout, but loses 1 Productivity this round.
events.flavor: A 20-minute breathing exercise squeezed between two deadlines.

events.name: Viral Hustle Culture Post
events.effect: Every player immediately resolves a Mandatory Training card, out of the normal cycle.
events.flavor: ‘Sleep is for people who don't want it badly enough’ — 2.1M likes.

events.name: A Colleague's Job Interview (Elsewhere)
events.effect: Each player chooses: gain 2 Political Capital (you covered for them), or gain 2 Productivity (you quietly took their tasks).
events.flavor: They ‘have a dentist appointment.’ Everyone knows.

events.name: Open-Floor-Plan Renovation
events.effect: Every player gains 1 Burnout.
events.flavor: Now you can hear every phone call within 40 feet. Great for focus.

events.name: Catered Lunch (Actually Good This Time)
events.effect: Every player gains 1 Productivity and 1 Political Capital.
events.flavor: The good taco place. Morale is measurably higher.

events.name: Server Room Overheats
events.effect: Whoever has the most Skill cards in play loses 1 Productivity.
events.flavor: It's always the one player with the biggest tableau.

events.name: The Standing Desk Convert
events.effect: Each player who owns “Standing Desk Enthusiast” gains 1 extra Political Capital.
events.flavor: They will tell you about it regardless.

events.name: The Spreadsheet Leaks Early
events.effect: Every player gains 1 Political Capital.
events.flavor: Someone found next Quarter's ranking spreadsheet. Everyone's recalculating.

events.name: Everything Is On Fire (Metaphorically, Probably)
events.effect: Whoever has the most Burnout gains 1 more Burnout. Every other player gains 1 Political Capital in sympathy.
events.flavor: It's always the same person's fire.

events.name: Therapy Benefit Actually Gets Used
events.effect: Any one player may remove 2 Burnout.
events.flavor: Covered at 60% after the deductible. Still worth it.

events.name: National Cybersecurity Awareness Month (It's Also October)
events.effect: Every player gains 1 Compliance Badge.
events.flavor: The phishing test email had 12 typos. 30% of the company still clicked it.

trainings.name: Anti-Harassment Training (The Annual Awkward One)
trainings.flavor: Same video. Same actor. Somehow one year older each time.

trainings.name: Cybersecurity Awareness: You Clicked the Phishing Link
trainings.flavor: The email was from ‘IT Depatrment.’ It had a countdown timer.

trainings.name: Data Privacy Training (Promises Retention By Lunch)
trainings.flavor: GDPR, CCPA, and six acronyms nobody in the room can define.

trainings.name: DEI Refresher Course
trainings.flavor: The same slide deck as last year, with a new date in the corner.

trainings.name: Fire Safety Procedures (Nobody Remembers)
trainings.flavor: The evacuation map on the wall is for a building that no longer exists.

trainings.name: The Team Retreat (Trust Falls Included)
trainings.flavor: Someone did not get caught. It has changed the team dynamic permanently.

trainings.name: Information Security Awareness Month
trainings.flavor: Coincidentally also National Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Nobody planned this. Everyone's annoyed regardless.

trainings.name: New Manager Training (“Good Luck” Sendoff)
trainings.effect: Gain 2 Compliance Badges instead of the usual 1.
trainings.flavor: Four hours on feedback techniques. Zero minutes on the raise you were promised.

trainings.name: Bias Workshop (Everyone Assumes It's About Someone Else)
Phishing Simulation Test (People Actually Failed)
trainings.effect: Standard. Additionally, whoever has the least Political Capital also gains 1 Burnout.

trainings.name: Ethics Training (Sponsored By the Thing That Caused It)
trainings.flavor: Scheduled the same week as the news story. Nobody mentions the news story.

trainings.name: Unconscious Bias Training, Redux
trainings.flavor: The sequel nobody asked for. Somehow longer than the original.

management.name: The Micromanager
management.effect: Your first Action each round must be Work a Project if you can afford one; otherwise it is wasted.
management.flavor: Wants a status update on the status update.

management.name: The Absentee Boss
management.effect: Gain 1 free Action Point each round. You may not take the Network action — your boss is never around to introduce you to anyone.
management.flavor: Hasn't reviewed a time-off request since the reorg. Or approved one. Or seen one.

management.name: The Credit-Stealing Boss
management.effect: Whenever you complete a Project, lose 1 Career Capital but gain 1 Political Capital (sympathetic coworkers notice).
management.flavor: Presented your work at the all-hands. Used the word ‘we’ a lot. Meant ‘I.’

management.name: The Chaotic Pivot-Happy Visionary
management.effect: At the start of each Quarter, flip a coin: heads, gain 2 Productivity; tails, discard 1 Skill card.
management.flavor: The strategy changed twice during this sentence.

management.name: The Yes-Man Exec
management.effect: Hiring Skill cards costs 1 less Productivity (minimum 1). Mandatory Training costs you 2 lost Action Points next round instead of 1.
management.flavor: Agreed with the last three people who talked to him. In the same meeting.

management.name: The Actually Supportive Manager
management.effect: Gain 1 Political Capital every Income Phase. No drawback.
management.flavor: Asked how you're doing and waited for the actual answer. Suspicious, but in a good way.

management.name: The Results-at-Any-Cost Boss
management.effect: Overtime grants +1 extra Productivity, but also +1 extra Burnout, on top of its normal effect.
management.flavor: Doesn't care how you hit the number. Cares extremely if you don't.

management.name: The Buzzword Machine
management.effect: Network grants +1 extra Political Capital. Your Projects cost 1 more Productivity — nobody can define the deliverable.
management.flavor: Wants to double-click on synergies before we boil the ocean.

management.name: The Founder Who Refuses to Delegate
management.effect: Gain 1 free Action Point each round. Self-Care costs 2 Action Points instead of 1.
management.flavor: “We're moving fast” has justified everything since 2019.

management.name: The Tenure-Not-Talent Manager
management.effect: Compliance Badges count double toward promotion requirements. Hiring Skill cards costs 1 more Productivity.
management.flavor: Been here 14 years. Still can't use the new expense software.

management.name: The Seagull Manager
management.effect: At the start of each Quarter, flip a coin: heads, every other player gains 1 Burnout — you swooped in and stirred things up; tails, you gain 1 Political Capital — you flew off before anyone noticed.
management.flavor: Flies in, makes a lot of noise, craps on the roadmap, and is gone before the retro.

management.name: The Mushroom Manager
management.effect: +2 Productivity/round (fed on nothing, somehow still growing); −1 Political Capital/round (kept in the dark — nobody tells you anything).
management.flavor: Kept in the dark and fed manure. Thriving, weirdly.

management.name: The Peter Principle
management.effect: Your Action Points are always 2, no matter your rung — promoted well past their competence. Hiring Skill cards costs 1 less Productivity (overcompensates by throwing tools at the problem).
management.flavor: Promoted three times. Still can't find the deploy script.

management.name: The Always-On Boss
management.effect: Overtime grants +1 extra Burnout, on top of its normal effect (always expects a same-night reply). Self-Care costs 2 Action Points instead of 1 (there's no such thing as fully logging off).
management.flavor: Texts you at 11 PM. Reacts with a 👍 to your out-of-office reply.

management.name: The Nepotism Hire
management.effect: +2 Political Capital/round (knows people); Hiring Skill cards costs 1 more Productivity (couldn't approve a headcount request to save their life).
management.flavor: Turns out the CEO is their uncle. Nobody has said this out loud.

management.name: The Consultant Turned Manager
management.effect: Your Projects cost 1 less Productivity (loves a framework for everything); −1 Political Capital/round (nobody trusts the person who charges by the hour).
management.flavor: Drew a 2x2 matrix. Nobody asked for the 2x2 matrix.

feedback.name: Exceeds Expectations
feedback.effect: +2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Hit every goal and then invented three more to hit. Nobody asked, but here we are.

feedback.name: Goes Above and Beyond
feedback.effect: +2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Answered a Slack at 2 a.m. once. It comes up in every review now.

feedback.name: A True Team Player
feedback.effect: +2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Brought donuts to the retro. The retro was about layoffs, but still.

feedback.name: Highly Visible Impact
feedback.effect: +2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: The dashboard is green. Nobody checks what it measures, but it's green.

feedback.name: Strong Executive Presence
feedback.effect: +2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Says 'let me push back on that' with total confidence and no follow-up.

feedback.name: Consistently Delivers
feedback.effect: +2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Delivers consistently, if not necessarily what was asked for.

feedback.name: Great Culture Add
feedback.effect: +2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Laughs at the CEO's jokes at exactly the right volume.

feedback.name: Promotion-Ready
feedback.effect: +2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Has been promotion-ready for six quarters. The ladder is just crowded.

feedback.name: The Team Depends on Them
feedback.effect: +2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Load-bearing employee. HR has flagged this as a risk, admiringly.

feedback.name: Needs to Improve Communication
feedback.effect: -2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Sends a 400-word Slack that could have been 'ok.' Or vice versa.

feedback.name: Struggles with Ambiguity
feedback.effect: -2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.

feedback.name: Not a Culture Fit
feedback.effect: -2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Logs off at 5. Suspicious. Un-American, even.

feedback.name: Lacks Executive Presence
feedback.effect: -2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Said 'I don't know' in a meeting. Out loud. On purpose.

feedback.name: Room for Growth
feedback.effect: -2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Endless, breathtaking room. Vistas of it. So much room.

feedback.name: Doesn't Take Feedback Well
feedback.effect: -2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Took the feedback fine. Just didn't agree, which is worse.

feedback.name: Siloed and Territorial
feedback.effect: -2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: Owns the one system nobody else understands. Weirdly protective of the job security.

feedback.name: Missed Key Deliverables
feedback.effect: -2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: The deliverable was 'vibes.' The vibes were, per the review, off.

feedback.name: Let's Circle Back on This
feedback.effect: -2 Political points during the Review to whoever holds this card.
feedback.flavor: We will not be circling back. We both know that.
